en: Thank you. Enjoy yourself, everybody. Welcome, welcome, welcome to the Tonight Show. This is it.
ko: 감사합니다. 즐기세요. 투나잇 쇼에 오신 걸 환영합니다.

en: You're here. Good night. Well, guys, let's get to some news. Today at the White House, President Trump hosted an event to celebrate Women's History Month.
ko: 오셨네요. 좋은 밤. 뉴스 보시죠. 오늘 백악관에서 트럼프가 여성 역사의 달 행사를 열었어요.

en: Yep, they discussed everything except Trump's history with women. -♪♪ Yeah, an hour into the event, Trump panicked because he didn't have an exit strategy for this, either.
ko: 여성과의 역사 빼고 다 논의했어요. 한 시간 만에 트럼프가 당황했어요. 여기도 출구 전략이 없어서요.

en: He's like, uh, what? Speaking of the war, um... After getting mixed messages, Trump was asked whether the U.S. involvement in Iran is a war or an excursion, and he said both.
ko: 뭐? 전쟁 얘기가 나왔는데. 이란 개입이 전쟁인지 소풍인지 물었더니 둘 다래요.

en: Yep, it's an excursion. That's also a war. It's like going to Ikea with your spouse. It's like, okay, really?
ko: 소풍이자 전쟁이래요. 배우자랑 이케아 가는 것 같은 거죠.

en: You buy it. You can't afford that. Meanwhile, today, Iran's new supreme leader issued his first public comments, and, weirdly, they were all about Donna Kelsey's home renovations.
ko: 그거 사, 살 여유 없어. 이란의 새 최고지도자가 첫 공개 발언을 했는데 도나 켈시 집 리모델링 얘기뿐이었어요.

en: Wow. Ha, ha, ha, ha. Well, guys, Trump announced that due to the oil shortage and high gas prices, he's tapping into our strategic reserves.
ko: 와. 트럼프가 석유 부족과 높은 유가 때문에 전략 비축유를 사용한다고 발표했어요.

en: Yeah, in fact, he just put out a video about it. Did you see this? Watch this. This is your president, and I don't want anyone worrying about the oil crisis because I've tapped our reserves and can guarantee one thing.
ko: 이 영상 보셨어요? 대통령입니다. 석유 위기 걱정 마세요. 비축유를 사용해서 한 가지를 보장합니다.

en: I have gas. In fact, they're saying I have more gas than anyone, no matter the situation.
ko: 가스가 있어요. 어떤 상황에서든 가스가 가장 많대요.

en: I have gas. In a quiet meeting? Gas. During a speech? Gas. Asleep? That's when I have the most gas.
ko: 가스가 있어요. 조용한 회의? 가스. 연설 중? 가스. 잠잘 때? 가스가 가장 많아요.

en: And good news for all you tree-huggers, my gas also creates incredible wind power. So, come on, America, follow me, and we'll let it rip.
ko: 환경운동가들에게 좋은 소식, 제 가스는 놀라운 풍력도 만들어요. 미국이여, 같이 가자.

en: Oh, my God. There he is. Oh, my God.
ko: 세상에. 저기 있네요. 세상에.

en: Did you guys see this? Yesterday, Trump gave a speech in Kentucky, and at one point, he brought Jake Paul up on stage.
ko: 이거 보셨어요? 트럼프가 켄터키 연설에서 제이크 폴을 무대에 올렸어요.

en: Oh. Yeah, and when Jake realized Trump is 79 years old, he challenged him to a boxing match.
ko: 제이크가 트럼프가 79세라는 걸 알고 복싱 시합을 신청했어요.

en: Look at that. Come on. Well, this is going viral.
ko: 봐봐요. 이게 바이럴이에요.

en: North Korean state media released a photo of Kim Jong-un test-firing a handgun. Look at this.
ko: 북한 관영 매체가 김정은의 권총 시험 발사 사진을 공개했어요.

en: There he is, Kim Jong-wik. Well, guys, spring break is in full swing, but if you still haven't booked that perfect trip, because a lot of popular destinations are rolling out new commercials to win you over, take a look at this one.
ko: 저기 있네요. 봄방학인데 아직 여행을 안 잡았으면 인기 여행지들이 새 광고를 내고 있어요.

en: If you want to catch up on sleep, to get tan, and to play golf, then check out President Trump on a Workday.
ko: 잠 자고 썬탠하고 골프 치고 싶으면 근무일의 트럼프 대통령을 확인하세요.

en: Hey, no, no, no, no, come on, hey, let's release the real spring break ad, please, come on.
ko: 야, 진짜 봄방학 광고 보여줘.

en: If you want pina coladas, Mai Tais, and spicy margaritas, then check out Pete Hegseth on a Workday.
ko: 피나콜라다, 마이타이, 매운 마가리타를 원하면 근무일의 피트 헥세스를 확인하세요.

en: Hey, hey, hey, well, no, no, no, no, come on, really, let's try this one more time. Please, play the real spring break commercial.
ko: 야, 진짜로 한 번만 더.

en: If you want no shirts, no shoes, no problems, then check out RFK Jr. on a Workday.
ko: 셔츠 없고 신발 없고 걱정 없고 싶으면 근무일의 RFK 주니어를 확인하세요.

en: All right, forget it, forget it, we'll get it, we'll do it next week, I don't care. We got time. Come on, it's the right video.
ko: 됐어, 다음 주에 하자.

en: I can't believe that. Can't believe it. Hey, have you guys heard about this?
ko: 못 믿겠다. 이거 들어보셨어요?

en: There's a new TikTok trend where runners are taking Ziploc bags filled with heavy cream and salt on their runs and making butter.
ko: 달리기하면서 지퍼백에 생크림이랑 소금 넣고 버터를 만드는 틱톡 트렌드가 있대요.

en: If you thought your coworker who ran a marathon was annoying, wait until you meet a guy who churns butter while running. Can't believe I'm saying this, but I miss the old days when people were just slapping each other with tortillas.
ko: 마라톤 뛴 동료가 짜증났으면 달리면서 버터 만드는 사람을 만나봐요. 토르티야로 서로 때리던 시절이 그리워요.

en: Well, get this, Apple is introducing eight new emojis in the latest iOS update. Here to talk about these new emojis is the president of the Emoji Safety Commission, Tim Stinson.
ko: 애플이 최신 iOS에 새 이모지 8개를 추가해요. 이모지 안전위원회 위원장 팀 스틴슨입니다.

en: Welcome to the show, Tim. Thank you. Thank you. Thank you for having me on, Jimmy. Look, these new emojis are out, and I am horrified.
ko: 팀, 환영합니다. 감사합니다. 새 이모지가 나왔는데 소름 끼쳐요.

en: Really? Why? They seem harmless. Are you kidding? These emojis are disgusting.
ko: 왜요? 무해해 보이는데. 농담이에요? 역겨워요.

en: I mean, you won't even be able to show them on TV. I think you can. I mean, look, here's the first one here.
ko: TV에서 못 보여줄 거예요. 보여줄 수 있을 것 같은데. 트롬본이에요.

en: Look, it's just a trombone. Oh, oh, okay. Can you say bone louder, Jimmy?
ko: bone을 크게 말해봐요. 애들이 문자로 이걸 보내면서 생각하는 거예요.

en: Because that's what the kids are thinking when they send this on their text phones. Oh, the blowing and the pulling, the tugging on the phone.
ko: 불고 당기고 잡아당기는 거요.

en: Hey, hey, hey, hey, hey, hey. You got to blur it. You got to blur that, Jimmy. Yeah, you might have to blur that.
ko: 야야야. 모자이크 해. 모자이크 해야 돼요.

en: Oh, look, how about this one? Oh, big foot. Are you kidding me?
ko: 이건 어때요? 빅풋이요. 농담이지?

en: What? What do they say about big feet, Jimmy? Big shoes?
ko: 뭐? 큰 발이면 뭐가 크다? 큰 신발?

en: Nope. No, no, no. Too late. Everyone's already thinking about it now, so you got to blur it. Blur it. Come on.
ko: 안 돼. 다들 이미 생각하고 있으니까 모자이크 해.

en: Okay, how about this one? Oh, oh, oh. What? No. It's just like a rock to me.
ko: 이건요? 바위 같은데.

en: Sure, sure. What rhymes with rock? Starts with a C?
ko: 뭐가 rock이랑 운이 맞아? C로 시작하는?

en: Come on, dude. Yeah, bingo. Blur it. Blur it. Stop yelling, blur it.
ko: 야. 맞아. 모자이크 해. 소리 지르지 마.

en: What's going on? Who are you yelling? Who are you yelling, dude? How about these right here? These are... Nope.
ko: 뭐야 이게? 누구한테 소리지르는 거야? 이건? 안 돼.

en: Nuh-uh. Nuh-uh. No, no. Booty and pole dancer. No, pole dancer is just a regular dancer.
ko: 안 돼. 부티랑 폴댄서. 폴댄서는 그냥 댄서야.

en: Yeah, oh, you don't know her story, Jimmy. Double blur it. Double blur. Okay, I think you're the one that's making these dirty, Tim.
ko: 그녀의 사연을 몰라잖아. 이중 모자이크. 팀, 당신이 더럽게 만드는 거잖아요.

en: You just really need to calm down. No, no, no, no, no. As the president of the Emoji Safety Commission, I'm only getting started.
ko: 진정하세요. 아니야. 이모지 안전위원회 위원장으로서 이제 시작이야.

en: You think I'm going soft on this, buddy? I'm only getting harder. Tim.
ko: 내가 약해지는 것 같아? 점점 세지고 있어. 팀.

en: Oh, my God. Oh, blur me. Blur me. Okay. Tim Stinson, everybody.
ko: 세상에. 저를 모자이크 해주세요. 팀 스틴슨이었습니다.

en: It's not a trombone. It's not a trombone, Jimmy. Don't let him fool you. Tim Stinson.
ko: 트롬본이 아니야, 지미. 속지 마. 팀 스틴슨.

en: Oh, my God. Ha. Trombone.
ko: 세상에. 트롬본이래.

en: Guys, I read about a group of Girl Scouts who started selling cookies outside a weed dispensary. And today, they just earned their billionaire merit badge.
ko: 걸스카우트들이 대마초 판매점 앞에서 쿠키를 팔기 시작했대요. 억만장자 공로 배지를 받았대요.

en: There you go. That's right.
ko: 그렇죠. 맞아요.
